overview: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) is a flagship Housing for All mission launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) on 25.06.2015 to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to urban poor, including Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Group (MIG). The mission operates through four verticals: Beneficiary-led Individual House Construction/Enhancement (BLC), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), In-situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), and Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS). PMAY-U 2.0 was launched in September 2024 to support an additional 1 crore families, with over 10 lakh houses sanctioned under PMAY-U 2.0 as of January 2026.
objectives: Provide pucca houses with basic amenities to urban poor
Empower women by ensuring house ownership in the name of female member or joint name
Address housing requirements of slum dwellers, EWS, LIG, and MIG categories in urban areas
Promote convergence with allied welfare schemes like PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
Facilitate home loans through Bank/HFC linkages with credit guarantee under CRGFTLIH
Promote use of innovative construction technologies, thermal comfort, and green/sustainable processes
Accelerate grounding, completion, and occupancy of houses under PMAY-U and PMAY-U 2.0
Reach out to vulnerable sections and Special Focus Groups for wider scheme coverage
eligibility: A beneficiary family under the Mission comprises of husband, wife, unmarried sons and/or unmarried daughters. The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house (an all-weather dwelling unit) either in his/her name or in the name of any member of his/her family in any part of India. An adult earning member (irrespective of marital status) is treated as a separate household provided that: He/she does not own a pucca (an all-weather dwelling unit) house in his/her name in any part of India. In case of a married couple, either of the spouses or both together in joint ownership will be eligible for a single house, subject to income eligibility of the household under the scheme. An eligible beneficiary can take advantage of only one of the four verticals of the Mission. Further, such person with pucca house having built-up area less than 21 sq.m may be included for enhancement of existing dwelling units upto 30 sq.m. However, if enhancement is not possible on account of lack of availability of land/space or any other reason, she/he may get a house under PMAY(U) elsewhere.
benefits: Central Assistance of ₹1.5 lakh is provided to individual eligible families belonging to EWS categories under BLC vertical. Central Assistance of ₹1.5 lakh per EWS house is provided by Government of India in AHP projects where at least 35% of the houses are for EWS category and a single project has at least 250 houses. Slum redevelopment grant of ₹1 lakh per house is admissible for all houses built for eligible slum dwellers under ISSR vertical. Under CLSS, interest subsidy of 6.5%, 4% and 3% on loan amount upto ₹6 lakh, ₹9 lakh and ₹12 lakh were admissible for the eligible beneficiaries belonging to EWS/LIG, MIG-I and MIG-II respectively seeking housing loans from Banks, Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions. All houses to have drinking water, power supply, sewerage and toilet facility. Preference to persons with disabilities, ST/SC/OBCs, Minorities, Transgender and women (with overriding priority to widow).
financial_support: Under PMAY-U, Central Assistance is provided to States/UTs for addressing housing requirements. The Mission is implemented as a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) except the CLSS vertical which is implemented as a Central Sector Scheme. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode is used for transfer of Central Assistance and State/ULB share to beneficiaries of the BLC vertical where instalments are credited directly into the beneficiary’s bank account as per the geo-tagged stage of construction with PFMS and GIS based MIS. For CLSS, interest subsidy is credited upfront to the loan account of beneficiaries through Primary Lending Institutions resulting in reduced effective housing loan and Equated Monthly Instalment (EMI). The Net Present Value (NPV) of the interest subsidy is calculated at a discount rate of 9%.
grant_amount_per_entity: ₹1.5 lakh per house under BLC and AHP verticals for EWS category; ₹1 lakh per house under ISSR vertical; interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh per house under CLSS for EWS/LIG beneficiaries
application_process: 1. Visit the PMAY-U website at https://pmay-urban.gov.in/ 2. Click on 'Apply for PMAY-U 2.0' 3. Fill in the application form with personal and household details 4. Upload required documents such as identity proof, income proof, and property details 5. Submit the application and await verification by the concerned Urban Local Body (ULB) 6. Upon approval, the applicant will be registered as a beneficiary and can proceed with house construction or enhancement 7. For CLSS beneficiaries, apply through Primary Lending Institutions (PLIs) with loan application and required documents 8. Track application status via CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) at https://pmayuclap.gov.in/ 9. Receive SMS alerts at different stages of application processing 10. After subsidy approval, the amount is credited upfront to the loan account
required_documents: Identity proof (Aadhaar card, Voter ID, PAN card, etc.)
Income proof (salary slips, income tax returns, etc.)
Property details (ownership documents, encumbrance certificate, etc.)
Bank account details for DBT transfer
Affidavit declaring no pucca house ownership
For CLSS: loan application documents, property papers, income proof
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted year-round as per the scheme guidelines and notifications from time to time.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA), Government of India
contact_details: Email: not explicitly stated in evidence | Helpline: CLSS Toll-Free Helpline Numbers NHB: 1800-11-3377, 1800-11-3388; HUDCO: 1800-11-6163; SBI: 1800-11-2018
caveats: Under the Mission, beneficiaries can take advantage under one component only.
The validity of CLSS vertical was up to 31.03.2022.
The credit linked subsidy will be available only for loan amounts upto Rs 6 lakhs and additional loans beyond Rs. 6 lakhs, if any, will be at nonsubsidized rate.

The carpet area of houses being constructed under this component of the mission should be upto 30 square metres and 60 square metres for EWS and LIG, respectively in order to avail of this credit linked subsidy.
Interest subsidy would be limited to first Rs. 6 lakh only even if the beneficiary builds a house of larger area.
